section.title: Dashboard
section.overview: The Dashboard provides a real-time, comprehensive overview of your eQMS system health. Monitor critical KPIs, recent activity across all modules, and tasks requiring your immediate attention — all from a single screen.

features.title: KPI Widgets
features.description: Real-time display of critical metrics including pending document approvals, open CAPAs, overdue tasks, and training compliance rates.

features.title: Priority Tasks
features.description: A prioritized list of tasks requiring immediate attention, sorted by urgency and due date for efficient workload management.

features.title: Recent Activity Feed
features.description: A live log of the latest system actions, helping you quickly grasp what is happening across all modules and teams.

features.title: Alerts & Reminders
features.description: Proactive notifications for upcoming deadlines, expiring documents, past-due calibrations, and incomplete training assignments.

steps.title: Review KPI Widgets
steps.description: Scan the KPI widget area at the top of the screen for a high-level summary of system health and outstanding items.

steps.title: Check Pending Tasks
steps.description: Review the My Tasks panel directly on the dashboard, or click 'View All' to navigate to the full My Tasks page.

steps.title: Monitor Recent Activity
steps.description: Browse the activity feed to see who performed which actions in the system and when they occurred.

steps.title: Act on Alerts
steps.description: Click any alert to be navigated directly to the relevant record that requires your attention.

tips.text: Dashboard widgets can be rearranged by drag-and-drop to suit your personal workflow preferences.
Dashboard data refreshes automatically every 5 minutes. Click the Refresh button to update the view immediately.

section.title: Document Control
section.overview: The Document Control module is the backbone of eQMS, managing every stage of the document lifecycle — from creation, drafting, review, and approval, through to official issuance and archival. It ensures all controlled documents are traceable, current, and compliant with your organization's processes.

features.title: Create & Draft
features.description: Create new documents from pre-built templates or from scratch, with full metadata fields, an integrated editor, and configurable approval workflows.

features.title: Review & Approval Workflow
features.description: Documents follow a configurable Review → Approval process secured by electronic signatures (e-Signature) to ensure authenticity at every step.

features.title: Version Management
features.description: Maintain a complete version history for every document, compare differences between revisions, and roll back to any previous version if needed.

features.title: Controlled Copies
features.description: Distribute Controlled Copies to relevant departments, track their distribution status, and automatically recall them when a newer version is issued.

steps.title: Create a New Document
steps.description: Go to Documents > All Documents and click '+ New Document'. Fill in the required fields: title, document type, owning department, and the reviewer/approver list.

steps.title: Draft the Content
steps.description: Use the integrated document editor to write content and upload file attachments. Save as a draft at any time without triggering the approval workflow.

steps.title: Submit for Review
steps.description: When the draft is ready, click 'Submit for Review'. All assigned reviewers will receive an email notification.

steps.title: Review & Comment
steps.description: Reviewers read the document, add inline comments if revisions are needed, or click 'Approve' to advance it to the next stage.

steps.title: Approve & Issue
steps.description: The authorized approver signs the document with their e-Signature to issue it officially. The document status changes to Effective.

steps.title: Distribute Controlled Copies
steps.description: Generate Controlled Copies from the effective document and distribute them to all relevant departments or locations.

tips.text: Use document Templates to save significant time when creating multiple documents of the same type.
Only the document Owner can edit an existing document or initiate a new revision. Contact your Quality team to update ownership if needed.
Effective documents cannot be directly edited. A Document Revision must be created to propose any changes to an issued document.

section.title: Training Management
section.overview: The Training Management module provides end-to-end oversight of your organization's training program — from course planning and learning material management, to tracking employee completion and generating audit-ready training records that meet GMP and ISO requirements.

features.title: Course Management
features.description: Create and manage training courses with full details: learning objectives, target audience, schedule, reference materials, and minimum pass score.

features.title: Training Materials
features.description: Upload and manage learning assets (SOPs, videos, slides) that link directly to documents in the Document Control module for seamless traceability.

features.title: Training Matrix
features.description: A role-based compliance matrix displaying required training by department or job title against actual employee completion status, clearly highlighting gaps.

features.title: Training Records
features.description: Maintain a permanent training record for every employee, exportable as PDF or Excel for customer audits or regulatory authority submissions.

steps.title: Create a New Training Course
steps.description: Go to Training > Courses and click '+ New Course'. Enter course title, description, learning objectives, and passing criteria.

steps.title: Attach Learning Materials
steps.description: Link SOPs from Document Control, upload presentation slides, training videos, and add any external reference links.

steps.title: Assign Courses to Employees
steps.description: Go to Training > Assignments. Assign courses to specific employees, departments, or job roles. Set due dates and recurrence policy.

steps.title: Complete Training
steps.description: Employees receive notifications, access the assigned course, and complete a final test/quiz if configured.

steps.title: Verify Completion
steps.description: The system automatically records the training completion date and score. Authorized trainers can verify final qualification manually if needed.

steps.title: Generate Training Reports
steps.description: Access Training Reports to view completion statistics, identify gaps, and export audit-ready training record summaries.

tips.text: Set automatic recurrence for critical GMP training (e.g., every 12 months) to ensure continuous compliance.
Training records are permanent. Once marked as complete, the record cannot be deleted, only edited by Quality Managers.
Training completion triggers automatic notification to the employee's manager and quality department.

section.title: Deviations & NCs
section.overview: The Deviations module enables you to document, investigate, and resolve any departure from approved procedures or established standards. Proper deviation management ensures that non-conformances are addressed quickly and systematically, safeguarding product quality and regulatory compliance.

features.title: Capture & Classify
features.description: Record deviation events with complete details: description, root cause classification, severity, affected products, and related documents.

features.title: Investigation
features.description: Conduct formal investigations with cross-functional teams, document findings, and link results directly to CAPA actions as necessary.

features.title: Approval & Closure
features.description: All deviations must be formally reviewed, justified, and approved by Quality Assurance before being closed with full traceability.

features.title: Deviation Analytics
features.description: Track trend analysis over time to identify recurring issues, root cause patterns, and improvement opportunities.

steps.title: Report a Deviation
steps.description: Go to Deviations > All Deviations and click '+ New Deviation'. Provide a clear description, classification, and severity level.

steps.title: Immediate Action
steps.description: Document any containment actions taken immediately to prevent further non-conformance (e.g., quarantine affected product).

steps.title: Assign Investigation Team
steps.description: Assign QA, QC, and operations personnel to conduct the investigation. Set target completion dates.

steps.title: Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Use 5-Why, Fishbone Diagram, or Failure Mode analysis to identify the true root cause of the deviation.

steps.title: Link to CAPA
steps.description: If systemic improvement is needed, initiate a CAPA record directly from the deviation to ensure corrective action.

steps.title: QA Review & Closure
steps.description: Quality Assurance reviews all investigation data, approves the disposition, and formally closes the deviation.

tips.text: All critical deviations (impacting patient safety or product quality) must be reported to regulatory authorities as per local requirements.
Attach photos, lab test results, and batch records as evidence to support your investigation findings.
Deviations cannot be deleted once approved. Use the 'Archived' status to hide obsolete or cancelled deviation records.

section.title: CAPA Management
section.overview: The CAPA module provides structured workflows to manage Corrective Actions (resolving existing problems) and Preventive Actions (preventing potential problems). It ensures that root causes are eliminated, implemented solutions are verified, and effectiveness is validated before closure.

features.title: CAPA Initiation
features.description: Create CAPA records from deviations, audit findings, customer complaints, or proactive risk assessments, ensuring every issue is tracked.

features.title: Action Planning
features.description: Define specific, measurable action items with owners, due dates, and resource requirements to address identified root causes.

features.title: Implementation & Verification
features.description: Track action implementation progress and verify that each step has been correctly executed before proceeding to effectiveness check.

features.title: Effectiveness Review
features.description: Conduct a formal effectiveness review (typically 30–90 days post-implementation) to confirm that the CAPA has resolved the issue.

steps.title: Initiate CAPA
steps.description: Go to CAPA > All CAPAs and click '+ New CAPA'. Link it to the source event (deviation, audit finding, complaint, etc.).

steps.title: Define the Problem
steps.description: Clearly describe the problem statement, what happened, when it happened, and the impact to quality, safety, or compliance.

steps.title: Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Perform a root cause investigation (5 Whys, Fishbone, etc.) and document the findings within the CAPA record.

steps.title: Plan Actions
steps.description: Define Corrective and/or Preventive actions, assign owners, set due dates, and attach supporting documents as needed.

steps.title: Execute Actions
steps.description: Action owners implement the planned actions, update status, and attach evidence of completion (photos, data, etc.).

steps.title: Verify Implementation
steps.description: Quality team verifies that the actions were completed correctly and fully as planned.

steps.title: Effectiveness Check
steps.description: After a defined period, review data to confirm the CAPA resolved the issue. If not effective, re-plan and re-execute.

steps.title: Closure
steps.description: Once effectiveness is confirmed, the QA manager formally closes the CAPA with final approval.

tips.text: CAPA effectiveness reviews should be conducted at least 30 days after implementation to allow sufficient time to observe results.
Overdue CAPAs are automatically flagged on the Dashboard and escalated to upper management via email notifications.
All CAPA records are subject to regulatory inspection. Ensure all fields are complete and accurate before submitting for approval.

section.title: Change Controls
section.overview: The Change Control module provides formal governance for all changes to validated systems, manufacturing processes, facilities, or critical documents. It ensures that every change is evaluated for impact, approved by the appropriate stakeholders, and implemented in a controlled manner to maintain compliance and system integrity.

features.title: Change Request
features.description: Initiate change requests with full justification, risk assessment, and linked documentation to ensure transparency and traceability.

features.title: Impact Assessment
features.description: Perform cross-functional impact assessments covering quality, safety, regulatory compliance, and validation status.

features.title: Approval Workflow
features.description: Multi-level approvals from Quality Assurance, Operations, and Validation teams ensure every change is properly reviewed.

features.title: Implementation & Verification
features.description: Track implementation tasks, verify correct execution, and close the change request with formal sign-off.

steps.title: Initiate Change Request
steps.description: Go to Change Control > All Changes and click '+ New Change Request'. Provide a clear description, justification, and urgency level.

steps.title: Risk & Impact Assessment
steps.description: Complete the impact assessment form covering all affected areas: Quality, Safety, Validation, Training, and Documentation.

steps.title: Approval Routing
steps.description: Route the change request to QA, Operations, and any other required approvers as defined by your company's change control policy.

steps.title: Plan Implementation
steps.description: Once approved, create an implementation plan with specific tasks, owners, and target completion dates.

steps.title: Execute Change
steps.description: Implement the change according to the approved plan. Document all activities and attach evidence of completion.

steps.title: Verify & Close
steps.description: Verify that the change was executed correctly and the desired outcome was achieved. Close the change request with final QA sign-off.

tips.text: Changes to validated systems may require re-validation or supplemental validation work. Consult your Validation team before execution.
Use the 'Emergency Change' type for urgent changes that require immediate implementation, followed by post-implementation review.
All approved changes trigger automatic notifications to affected departments and personnel to ensure everyone is aware.

section.title: Complaints Management
section.overview: The Complaints module provides a systematic approach to receive, triage, investigate, and resolve customer complaints. It ensures timely response to customer concerns, links to CAPA for systemic improvements, and maintains complete traceability from receipt through final closure and regulatory reporting.

features.title: Complaint Logging
features.description: Record all customer complaints with full details: reporter information, product details, batch number, and complaint description.

features.title: Investigation
features.description: Conduct investigations involving QA, QC, and Operations to determine root cause and implement immediate corrective actions.

features.title: CAPA Linkage
features.description: Link complaints directly to CAPA records when systemic improvements are needed to prevent recurrence.

features.title: Trend Analysis
features.description: Analyze complaint trends by product, category, and root cause to identify patterns and proactively address emerging issues.

steps.title: Log Complaint
steps.description: Go to Complaints > All Complaints and click '+ New Complaint'. Enter customer information, product, batch, and complaint details.

steps.title: Triage & Classify
steps.description: Classify complaint severity (Critical, Major, Minor) and assign to the appropriate investigator (QA, QC, Technical).

steps.title: Conduct Investigation
steps.description: Investigate the complaint by reviewing batch records, testing data, and interviewing personnel. Document all findings.

steps.title: Determine Root Cause
steps.description: Use root cause analysis techniques (5-Why, Fishbone) to identify the underlying cause of the complaint.

steps.title: Implement Corrective Action
steps.description: If a systemic issue is found, initiate a CAPA record. For isolated incidents, document the immediate action taken.

steps.title: Customer Response
steps.description: Prepare and send a formal response letter to the customer explaining findings, actions taken, and timeline.

steps.title: Close Complaint
steps.description: Once all actions are complete and customer is satisfied, formally close the complaint with QA approval.

tips.text: Some complaints may require regulatory reporting (e.g., serious adverse events). Check your company's regulatory reporting policy.
Respond to customer complaints within your company's committed timeframe (typically 5-10 business days) to maintain customer satisfaction.
Complaint records are subject to customer audits. Ensure all investigation documentation is complete and accurate.

section.title: Risk Management
section.overview: The Risk Management module provides a structured, ICH Q9-aligned framework for identifying, assessing, controlling, and monitoring quality risks across your organization. It enables proactive risk-based decision-making — from product development and process changes to supplier management and regulatory compliance — ensuring risks are managed systematically and transparently.

features.title: Risk Identification
features.description: Capture and catalog potential risks using structured forms including hazard description, risk source, affected processes, and initial risk category classification.

features.title: Risk Assessment Matrix
features.description: Evaluate each risk using a configurable Severity × Probability × Detectability matrix to calculate a Risk Priority Number (RPN) and determine the risk level (Low / Medium / High / Critical).

features.title: Risk Controls & Mitigation
features.description: Define and assign risk mitigation actions with responsible owners, target dates, and effectiveness criteria. Link controls to CAPAs, Change Controls, or SOPs for full traceability.

features.title: Periodic Risk Review
features.description: Schedule recurring risk reviews to re-evaluate residual risk levels, verify control effectiveness, and update risk registers based on new data or process changes.

features.title: Risk Register & Reports
features.description: Maintain a centralized risk register with full audit history. Generate risk heat maps, trend reports, and management review summaries for informed decision-making.

steps.title: Create a Risk Assessment
steps.description: Go to Risk Management > Risk Register and click '+ New Risk'. Select the risk category, enter the hazard description, affected process or product, and initial observations.

steps.title: Evaluate Severity, Probability & Detectability
steps.description: Use the built-in Risk Assessment Matrix to score the risk on three dimensions: Severity of impact, Probability of occurrence, and Detectability. The system auto-calculates the RPN.

steps.title: Determine Risk Level & Accept or Mitigate
steps.description: Based on the RPN score, the system classifies the risk as Low, Medium, High, or Critical. Decide whether to accept the risk (with justification) or proceed with mitigation planning.

steps.title: Define Mitigation Actions
steps.description: For risks requiring mitigation, add specific action items: assign an owner, set a target completion date, and define measurable acceptance criteria for the residual risk level.

steps.title: Implement & Verify Controls
steps.description: Execute mitigation actions and document evidence of completion. Re-score the residual risk to confirm the RPN has been reduced to an acceptable level.

steps.title: Schedule Periodic Reviews
steps.description: Set up recurring review cycles (e.g., quarterly or annually) to reassess risks, verify ongoing control effectiveness, and update the risk register with any changes.

tips.text: Use risk categories aligned with ICH Q9 (e.g., Patient Safety, Product Quality, Data Integrity, Regulatory Compliance) for consistent risk classification across the organization.
Risks linked to CAPAs, Deviations, or Change Controls will automatically cross-reference, providing full traceability during regulatory inspections.
Critical and High risks require documented management approval before the risk can be accepted. Ensure escalation pathways are configured in system settings.
Export the Risk Heat Map before management review meetings to provide leadership with a visual overview of the organization's risk landscape.

section.title: Equipment Management
section.overview: The Equipment Management module oversees the entire lifecycle of critical manufacturing and laboratory equipment — from procurement and qualification, through regular maintenance and calibration, to decommissioning. It ensures equipment remains qualified, calibrated, and reliable to support compliant operations.

features.title: Equipment Master List
features.description: Maintain a centralized inventory of all equipment with full specifications, location, owner, and qualification status.

features.title: Maintenance Scheduling
features.description: Schedule preventive maintenance activities with automatic reminders and overdue alerts to ensure continuous equipment availability.

features.title: Calibration Management
features.description: Track calibration due dates, record calibration results, and automatically update equipment status based on pass/fail outcomes.

features.title: Equipment History
features.description: View complete maintenance, calibration, and repair history for every piece of equipment to support root cause investigations.

steps.title: Register Equipment
steps.description: Go to Equipment > Equipment List and click '+ Add Equipment'. Enter equipment ID, name, type, location, and responsible owner.

steps.title: Set Maintenance Schedule
steps.description: Define the preventive maintenance schedule (e.g., every 6 months) and assign maintenance tasks to the appropriate technician.

steps.title: Set Calibration Schedule
steps.description: For calibrated equipment, define the calibration frequency and link to calibration procedures or external providers.

steps.title: Perform Maintenance
steps.description: When maintenance is due, the system generates a task. Technicians complete the task and record all findings and actions taken.

steps.title: Perform Calibration
steps.description: Calibration personnel execute calibration per procedure, record results, and update equipment status to 'Calibrated' or 'Out of Calibration'.

steps.title: Review Equipment Status
steps.description: QA periodically reviews equipment status to ensure all critical equipment is qualified, calibrated, and available for use.

tips.text: Use the Equipment Dashboard to monitor upcoming maintenance and calibration due dates to prevent overdue situations.
Equipment that fails calibration must be immediately removed from service and tagged as 'Out of Calibration' to prevent inadvertent use.
Equipment qualification documents (IQ/OQ/PQ) should be linked to the equipment master record for easy reference during audits.

section.title: Supplier Management
section.overview: The Supplier Management module enables you to qualify, monitor, and maintain approved suppliers throughout their lifecycle. It ensures that all suppliers meet your quality standards, are periodically re-evaluated, and that their performance is tracked to support continuous improvement and risk mitigation.

features.title: Supplier Qualification
features.description: Conduct formal supplier qualification including audits, questionnaire responses, and risk assessments before approving suppliers.

features.title: Performance Monitoring
features.description: Track supplier performance metrics such as on-time delivery, quality defects, and complaint rates to identify reliable suppliers.

features.title: Audit Management
features.description: Schedule and conduct periodic supplier audits (on-site or remote), document findings, and track CAPA actions from audit results.

features.title: Re-Qualification
features.description: Automatically trigger re-qualification workflows based on time intervals, performance issues, or risk profile changes.

steps.title: Initiate Supplier Qualification
steps.description: Go to Supplier > All Suppliers and click '+ New Supplier'. Enter supplier details, type, and risk classification.

steps.title: Send Qualification Questionnaire
steps.description: Send the supplier qualification questionnaire via email and request supporting documents (certifications, quality agreements).

steps.title: Conduct Supplier Audit
steps.description: For high-risk suppliers, schedule and conduct an on-site or remote audit to verify compliance with your quality requirements.

steps.title: Review & Approve
steps.description: Quality Assurance reviews all qualification data and either approves the supplier or requests additional information.

steps.title: Monitor Performance
steps.description: Track supplier deliveries, quality metrics, and complaints. Periodically review performance scorecards.

steps.title: Conduct Re-Qualification
steps.description: Every 1-3 years (depending on risk), re-evaluate the supplier by repeating qualification steps and updating the approval status.

tips.text: Maintain a risk-based approach: high-risk suppliers require more frequent audits and stricter qualification criteria.
Suppliers that fail re-qualification must be removed from the Approved Supplier List and replaced to ensure continuous supply chain quality.
Supplier audit findings can be directly linked to CAPA records to ensure that supplier quality issues are systematically resolved.

section.title: Reports & Analytics
section.overview: The Reports module provides comprehensive data visualization and reporting capabilities across all eQMS modules. Generate custom reports, export data for regulatory submissions, and leverage interactive dashboards to gain insights into quality trends, compliance status, and operational performance.

features.title: Pre-Built Reports
features.description: Access a library of pre-configured reports covering documents, deviations, CAPA, training, and more — ready to use without customization.

features.title: Custom Report Builder
features.description: Build custom reports by selecting data sources, filters, grouping, and output format (PDF, Excel, CSV) tailored to your specific needs.

features.title: Scheduled Reports
features.description: Schedule reports to run automatically (daily, weekly, monthly) and deliver results via email to specific recipients.

features.title: Interactive Dashboards
features.description: Explore interactive charts and graphs to drill down into data, identify trends, and make data-driven quality decisions.

steps.title: Access Reports
steps.description: Go to Reports from the main menu. Browse available report categories or use the search function to find a specific report.

steps.title: Select Report Filters
steps.description: Choose filters such as date range, department, status, or document type to narrow down results to your area of interest.

steps.title: Preview Report
steps.description: Click 'Generate Preview' to view the report on-screen. Review data accuracy and adjust filters if needed.

steps.title: Export Report
steps.description: Export the report in your desired format (PDF for presentations, Excel for further analysis, CSV for data import).

steps.title: Schedule Automated Reports
steps.description: For recurring reports, click 'Schedule' to set frequency, email recipients, and delivery time.

steps.title: Create Custom Reports
steps.description: Use the Report Builder to design custom reports by selecting fields, applying filters, and configuring output layout.

tips.text: Save your frequently used filter combinations as 'Report Favorites' for faster access in the future.
Scheduled reports are automatically archived in the system and can be re-downloaded at any time from the Report History.
Exported reports contain sensitive data. Ensure files are stored securely and shared only with authorized personnel.

section.title: Audit Trail
section.overview: The Audit Trail module provides a comprehensive, tamper-proof log of all system activities. Every user action — including create, read, update, delete, and approval operations — is permanently recorded with timestamp, user identity, and the nature of the change. This ensures full traceability and meets 21 CFR Part 11 requirements for electronic records.

features.title: Complete Activity Log
features.description: Every system event is logged with date, time, user, action type, affected record, and before/after values for full transparency.

features.title: Advanced Search & Filter
features.description: Search audit logs by user, date range, module, action type, or record ID to quickly locate specific events during investigations.

features.title: Export for Audits
features.description: Export filtered audit logs to Excel or PDF format for internal audits, customer audits, or regulatory authority inspections.

features.title: Tamper-Proof Records
features.description: Audit logs are write-once, read-many (WORM) and cryptographically signed to prevent unauthorized modification or deletion.

steps.title: Access Audit Trail
steps.description: Go to Audit Trail from the main menu. The system displays the most recent activity log entries by default.

steps.title: Apply Filters
steps.description: Use filters to narrow results: select a specific user, date range, module (Documents, CAPA, etc.), or action type (Create, Edit, Delete, Approve).

steps.title: Review Event Details
steps.description: Click any log entry to view full details including timestamp, user, IP address, record ID, and the exact change made (before/after values).

steps.title: Export Audit Logs
steps.description: Select the filtered results and click 'Export' to generate an Excel or PDF report for audit submission or investigation records.

steps.title: Use Audit Trail for Investigations
steps.description: During deviation or CAPA investigations, reference the Audit Trail to reconstruct the timeline of events and identify who did what and when.

tips.text: Audit Trail records are retained permanently and cannot be edited or deleted by any user, including System Administrators.
Use the 'Changed Fields' filter to quickly identify all records where a specific field (e.g., Status, Approver) was modified.
Regulatory inspectors commonly request Audit Trail data. Ensure your team is trained on how to generate and interpret audit reports.

section.title: System Settings
section.overview: The Settings module provides all system administration capabilities: user account management, role-based access control, system configuration, data dictionary management, and technical system monitoring. Access is restricted to System Administrators and Quality Managers.

features.title: User Management
features.description: Create, edit, and deactivate user accounts. Manage user profiles and assign roles to precisely control each person's system access.

features.title: Roles & Permissions
features.description: Define roles with granular permission sets, configurable per module and per action type (Read/Create/Edit/Delete/Approve).

features.title: Configuration
features.description: Configure all system-wide settings: company branding, SMTP email server, password policies, session security, notification rules, and third-party integrations.

features.title: System Information
features.description: Monitor technical health indicators: application version, server resource utilization, database connectivity, API status, and the full system changelog.

steps.title: Manage Users
steps.description: Go to Settings > User Management to create new user accounts, assign or change roles, or issue a password reset. All accounts require a valid company email address.

steps.title: Configure Roles
steps.description: Design roles with the correct permission sets for each job function. Best practice: build roles around job titles rather than assigning permissions directly to individuals.

steps.title: System Configuration
steps.description: Go to Configuration to set the company logo, organization details, SMTP server settings, password complexity policies, and SSO/LDAP integration.

steps.title: Manage Dictionaries
steps.description: Configure all system dropdown values used across modules: Document Types, Departments, Retention Policies, and Storage Locations.

steps.title: Check System Information
steps.description: Periodically review System Information to proactively monitor server resource usage, database health, and the currently deployed application version.

tips.text: Always test all configuration changes in a staging environment before applying them to the production system.
Use the 'Roles Preview' feature to verify the exact permission set granted by a role before assigning it to any user account.
The System Administrator account must be protected with MFA and should only be used when strictly necessary for administrative tasks.
